name: Python
creator: Guido van Rossum
description: Python is a readable, versatile language used across web, data, automation, and scripting.
usedFor: Web development, data science, scripting, automation, machine learning.
funFact: Python is named after Monty Python, not the snake.

terminalCommands.install: Install from python.org or use your package manager; pip installs packages.

topics.title: Variables
topics.explanation: Variables store named values you can read and update in Python.
topics.beginnerExplanation: A variable is a named value holder.

keyTerms.definition: Storing a value in a name.

commonMistakes: Using a variable before it exists.

topics.title: Arrays/Collections
topics.explanation: Collections group multiple values for batch processing in Python.
commonMistakes: Assuming automatic growth when it does not happen.

topics.title: Loops
topics.explanation: Loops repeat code until a condition changes in Python.

topics.title: Functions
topics.explanation: Functions group reusable logic with inputs and outputs in Python.

topics.title: Conditionals
topics.explanation: Conditionals pick code paths based on tests in Python.
codeExample: print('A' if s>=90 else 'B' if s>=80 else 'C')

topics.title: Classes/Structs/Objects
topics.explanation: Types combine data and behavior in Python.

topics.title: Error Handling
topics.explanation: Errors are detected and handled safely in Python.

topics.title: File IO
topics.explanation: Programs read and write files in Python.

topics.title: Modules/Packages
topics.explanation: Code is split into reusable modules in Python.

topics.title: Decorators & Comprehensions
topics.explanation: An advanced Python feature used in production code.
codeExample: print([n*n for n in [1,2,3,4] if n%2==0])
challengeSolution: print([x for x in w if len(x)>3])

name: JavaScript
creator: Brendan Eich
description: JavaScript powers interactive web pages and server-side apps with a flexible, event-driven model.
usedFor: Front-end web, Node.js servers, mobile apps, tooling.
funFact: JavaScript was created in about 10 days at Netscape.

terminalCommands.install: Install Node.js from nodejs.org.

name: TypeScript
creator: Anders Hejlsberg
description: TypeScript adds static types to JavaScript and compiles to plain JS for safer large codebases.
usedFor: Large web apps, Angular/React codebases, Node services.
funFact: TypeScript is a strict superset of JavaScript.

terminalCommands.install: npm install -g typescript

topics.title: Variables
topics.explanation: Variables store named values you can read and update in TypeScript.
topics.beginnerExplanation: A variable is a named value holder.

keyTerms.definition: Storing a value in a name.

commonMistakes: Using a variable before it exists.

topics.title: Arrays/Collections
topics.explanation: Collections group multiple values for batch processing in TypeScript.
commonMistakes: Assuming automatic growth when it does not happen.

topics.title: Loops
topics.explanation: Loops repeat code until a condition changes in TypeScript.
codeExample: for(const n of [1,2,3] as number[]) t+=n;

topics.title: Functions
topics.explanation: Functions group reusable logic with inputs and outputs in TypeScript.

topics.title: Conditionals
topics.explanation: Conditionals pick code paths based on tests in TypeScript.

topics.title: Classes/Structs/Objects
topics.explanation: Types combine data and behavior in TypeScript.
codeExample: class Dog{constructor(public name:string){} speak(){return this.name+' woof';}}

topics.title: Error Handling
topics.explanation: Errors are detected and handled safely in TypeScript.

name: C
creator: Dennis Ritchie
description: C is a low-level systems language that influenced most modern languages.
usedFor: Operating systems, embedded systems, performance-critical libraries.
funFact: Unix was rewritten in C.

terminalCommands.install: Install GCC (MinGW on Windows) or Clang via your package manager.

topics.title: Variables
topics.explanation: Variables in C are named memory locations. You must declare the type of variable before using it.
topics.beginnerExplanation: Variables are named typed storage in C.

keyTerms.definition: Specifies the variable name and type.
Assigning a value at the time of declaration.

topics.challenge: Declare an integer variable 'age' and assign it 21.

topics.title: Arrays/Collections
topics.explanation: Arrays store multiple values of the same type in contiguous memory locations.
topics.beginnerExplanation: Arrays hold fixed-size sequences in C.

keyTerms.definition: A collection of variables of the same type.
Position of element in the array, beginning from 0.

topics.title: Loops
topics.explanation: Loops are used to repeat a block of code for a certain number of times or until a condition is met.
topics.beginnerExplanation: Loops repeat code in C using for/while.

keyTerms.definition: Repeats code a specific number of times.
Repeats code while a condition holds.

topics.challenge: Print numbers 1 to 5 using a loop.

topics.title: Functions
topics.explanation: Functions group code into reusable units. You must declare a return type and parameters.
topics.beginnerExplanation: Functions group reusable steps in C.

keyTerms.definition: Named section of code that performs a task.
Variable used to receive data inside function.

topics.challenge: Write a function multiply that returns product of two ints.
topics.challengeHint: Return int, take two int parameters.

topics.title: Conditionals
topics.explanation: Conditionals in C use if, else if, and else to branch based on comparisons.
topics.beginnerExplanation: This lesson covers conditionals in C.

keyTerms.definition: Runs code when a condition is true.

topics.title: Classes/Structs/Objects
topics.explanation: Structs are custom data types that group variables under one name.
topics.beginnerExplanation: Structs group related fields in C.
codeExample: struct Point { int x; int y; };
solution: struct Car { char model[20]; int year; };
commonMistakes: Accessing members with dot operator incorrectly.
topics.challenge: Declare a struct Student with name and grade.
topics.challengeHint: Use char array for name, int for grade.
challengeSolution: struct Student { char name[50]; int grade; };

topics.title: Error Handling
topics.explanation: C signals errors with return codes and errno; always check results from library calls.
topics.beginnerExplanation: This lesson covers error handling in C.

keyTerms.definition: 0 often means success; non-zero means failure.
Global error indicator set by many C library functions.

topics.expectedOutput: open failed: No such file or directory

topics.title: File IO
topics.explanation: C allows reading and writing files using FILE pointer and functions like fopen, fprintf, fscanf.
topics.beginnerExplanation: C reads/writes files with FILE pointers.

keyTerms.definition: Pointer to structure controlling a file.

commonMistakes: Not checking if file opened successfully.
topics.challenge: Open a file data.txt for appending.

topics.title: Modules/Packages
topics.explanation: The C preprocessor runs before compilation, handling tasks like including files, defining macros, and conditional code.
topics.beginnerExplanation: Headers and includes reuse C code across files.
topics.challenge: Define a macro SQUARE(x) that multiplies x by itself.

topics.title: Pointers & Memory
topics.explanation: A pointer is a variable that stores the memory address of another variable.
topics.beginnerExplanation: Pointers track where data lives in memory.

keyTerms.definition: Variable that holds a memory address.
Access the value a pointer points to.

commonMistakes: Using wrong types in pointer assignment.
topics.challenge: Declare a pointer to int named 'iptr'.

name: C++

usedFor: Game engines, high-performance software, systems tooling.

solution: ifstream i("n.txt"); string s; getline(i,s); cout<<s<<endl;
challengeSolution: ifstream i("n.txt"); string s; getline(i,s); cout<<s<<endl;

name: Assembly
creator: Various
description: Assembly language maps closely to CPU instructions for maximum control.
usedFor: Bootloaders, drivers, reverse engineering, tight embedded loops.
funFact: Each CPU family has its own assembly dialect.

terminalCommands.windows: nasm -f win64 file.asm && link file.obj /subsystem:console /entry:main file.exe
terminalCommands.mac: nasm -f macho64 file.asm && ld -lSystem -o file file.o
terminalCommands.linux: nasm -f elf64 file.asm && ld file.o -o file && ./file
terminalCommands.install: Install NASM and a linker; exact steps vary by OS.

function Dog:speak() return self.name..' woof' end

topics.title: Metatables
topics.explanation: An advanced Lua feature used in production code.
solution: local mt={__tostring=function() return 'X' end}; local x=setmetatable({},mt); print(tostring(x))
challengeSolution: local mt={__add=function(x,y) return {v=x.v+y.v} end}; local a=setmetatable({v=1},mt); local b=setmetatable({v=2},mt); print((a+b).v)

topics.explanation: Code is split into reusable modules in PHP.
solution: namespace App; class Hi{} echo Hi::class;

topics.title: Traits & Namespaces
topics.explanation: An advanced PHP feature used in production code.
solution: trait T{public function id(){return 1;}} class C{use T;} echo (new C())->id();
challengeSolution: trait A{function a(){return 'A';}} class X{use A;} echo (new X())->a();

name: Bash
creator: Brian Fox
description: Bash is the default shell on many Linux and macOS systems.
usedFor: Automation, DevOps scripts, system administration.
funFact: Bash is the GNU Bourne Again Shell.

terminalCommands.install: Preinstalled on macOS/Linux; use Git Bash or WSL on Windows.

topics.title: Variables
topics.explanation: Variables store named values you can read and update in Bash.
topics.beginnerExplanation: A variable is a named value holder.

keyTerms.definition: Storing a value in a name.

commonMistakes: Using a variable before it exists.

topics.title: Arrays/Collections
topics.explanation: Collections group multiple values for batch processing in Bash.
commonMistakes: Assuming automatic growth when it does not happen.

topics.title: Loops
topics.explanation: Loops repeat code until a condition changes in Bash.
codeExample: for n in 1 2 3; do t=$((t+n)); done
solution: for i in {1..3}; do echo $i; done
challengeSolution: for i in {1..5}; do echo $i; done

topics.title: Functions
topics.explanation: Functions group reusable logic with inputs and outputs in Bash.

topics.title: Conditionals
topics.explanation: Conditionals pick code paths based on tests in Bash.
codeExample: if [ $s -ge 90 ]; then echo A; elif [ $s -ge 80 ]; then echo B; else echo C; fi
solution: if (( n%2==0 )); then echo even; else echo odd; fi
challengeSolution: if (( n%2==0 )); then echo even; else echo odd; fi

topics.title: Classes/Structs/Objects
topics.explanation: Types combine data and behavior in Bash.

topics.explanation: Errors are detected and handled safely in Bash.
